title: duroia macrophylla
intro: Duroia macrophylla, also known as the "water dog" or "water tiger," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and treating wounds. Duroia macrophylla is often prepared for herbal use in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory issues to gastrointestinal problems.
